bead.title: agent: close parents after auto epic decomposition
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The auto-epic decomposition path also uses the legacy park-open pattern. Even if pre-claim and recovery decomposition close parents correctly, auto epic decomposition can still leave a losslessly decomposed parent open and execution-ineligible.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go:182-204 uses the same park-open behavior for auto epic decomposition instead of closing the decomposed parent.
- Existing coverage does not require the auto epic path to close the parent while retaining `auto_decompose_attempted` evidence.

PROPOSED FIX
Update `AutoDecomposeEpic` in cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go so, after generated children are durably created, the decomposed epic parent is closed through the lifecycle API as completed-by-decomposition. Preserve `auto_decompose_attempted` evidence and include child IDs in durable event/extra evidence. Clear claim metadata and heartbeat from the parent on close. Preserve child readiness semantics: Parent metadata is traceability only and must not create a dependency gate.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement the broader `parent_ancestor_in_deps` doctor/back-edge repair slice tracked by ddx-130cf9ab. This bead must not regress it and should reuse its invariant, but it does not replace that repair/backfill work.
- Do not redesign epic auto-close or historical `dead_intermediate_close`; keep those as legacy/backfill behavior for already-open containers.
- Do not drain or close existing decomposed queue entries as part of this bead. File or run a separate reconciliation bead if historical tracker cleanup is needed.
- Do not change Fizeau routing, model escalation, or provider selection.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b.
Related, not dependency: ddx-130cf9ab.
Governing artifacts: TD-031, SD-025, ADR-023, ADR-024, FEAT-010.

PARENT AND DEPS
Parent: ddx-2a1ece2b.
Deps: No deps. The related ddx-130cf9ab work can land independently; this child must include no-parent-dep assertions so it is safe either before or after that repair slice.
bead.acceptance:
1. `AutoDecomposeEpic` in cli/internal/agent/auto_decompose_epic.go closes the decomposed parent through the lifecycle API after children are created.
2. The auto epic path still emits `auto_decompose_attempted` evidence and records generated child IDs in durable event/extra evidence.
3. The closed parent has claim metadata and heartbeat cleared.
4. Generated auto epic children carry `Parent=<decomposed-id>` only as hierarchy metadata and do not include the decomposed parent in `DepIDs()`.
5. `TestAutoDecomposeEpic_HappyPathClosesParent` or equivalent proves the auto-epic-decompose path closes the parent, clears claim metadata/heartbeat, leaves generated children ready when they have no explicit deps, and still emits `auto_decompose_attempted` evidence.
6. `TestDecomposeDoesNotSelfDep` or equivalent covers auto epic decomposition children or a dedicated `TestAutoDecomposeEpic_DoesNotDependOnParent` asserts every generated child excludes the parent ID from `DepIDs()`.
7. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... -run 'AutoDecomposeEpic|DecomposeDoesNotSelfDep|parent_ancestor_in_deps' -count=1` passes.
8. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./internal/bead/... ./cmd/... -count=1` passes.
9. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
